title: Euodia pteleifolia
intro: Euodia pteleifolia, also known as Chinese Euodia, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential effects on cardiovascular and immune system health. The bioactive constituents of Euodia pteleifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also employed in the production of traditional Chinese medicine formulas, such as those used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Euodia pteleifolia is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family consists of flowering plants with distinctive fragrances and essential oils. Euodia pteleifolia is further classified in the genus Euodia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. The exact classification of Euodia pteleifolia may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a species of shrub or small tree. The classification of Euodia pteleifolia is also closely related to that of other plants in the Rutaceae family.
